description: Export a complete character build to JSON and re-apply it to a new character. Covers all aspects of character identity that are race/gender neutral.

export.description: Return all currently equipped items by biped slot with FormIDs and enchantment data

slots.slotName: string (head, body, hands, feet, ring, amulet, shield, leftHand, rightHand, ammo, etc.)

item.name: string
item.tempering: string (none, fine, superior, exquisite, flawless, epic, legendary)

export.implementation: Iterate RE::PlayerCharacter biped slots (0-31). For each slot, get equipped TESForm, read base object, enchantment via EnchantmentItem, tempering level from extra data list.

export.description: Return all spells the player has learned, categorized by school and type
export.implementation: Use RE::PlayerCharacter::GetActorBase()->GetSpellList() or iterate AddedSpells. Filter out race abilities and innate spells unless flagged.

export.description: Return all shouts and words of power the player has unlocked
export.implementation: Iterate player's unlocked shouts via TESShout forms. Check word unlock status per word of power.

export.description: Return all 18 skill levels with current XP progress
export.implementation: For each of the 18 skills, call GetBaseActorValue() on PlayerCharacter. For XP progress, access the skill data from PlayerSkills.

export.description: Return all perks the player has acquired, including rank

perks.name: string
perks.skill: string (which skill tree it belongs to, or 'none' for standalone)

export.implementation: Iterate player's perk list via GetPerks() or ActorBase perk array. For each BGSPerk, get name, FormID, and current rank. Available perk points from game global.

export.description: Return all active magical effects with remaining duration. Existing tool returns basic data — needs enchantment source detail.
export.enhancement: Add source item/spell that caused the effect, distinguish between enchantment passive effects and timed spell effects.

import.notes: Already working. Used to add equipment, weapons, ammo, soul gems, gold.
Listed in roadmap as implemented but not tested this session. Need to verify.
Listed in roadmap as implemented but not tested this session. Need to verify.
Listed in roadmap as implemented but not tested this session. Need to verify.

import.description: Unlock a specific word of power / shout for the player
import.implementation: Use player.UnlockWord() or teach shout via console command 'player.teachword <formId>' + 'player.unlockword <formId>'.

sourceCharacter.name: string
sourceCharacter.note: Reference only — not applied on import

importWorkflow.description: Step-by-step process for applying a blueprint to a new character

steps.details: For each of the 18 skills, set to the exported level value. Must be done BEFORE perks since some perks have skill level requirements.
steps.errorHandling: If a skill level exceeds vanilla max (100), may require uncapper mod. Skip and log warning.

steps.details: Add each perk by FormID. Process in dependency order if possible (lower-tier perks first).
steps.errorHandling: If FormID fails (mod not loaded), skip and log. Some perks depend on skill levels set in step 1.

steps.details: Add each spell by FormID. Filter by source — try vanilla/DLC spells first, then modded.
steps.errorHandling: If FormID fails (mod not loaded), skip and log. Spells from uninstalled mods will fail silently.

steps.details: For each shout, teach all words and unlock them. May need player.teachword + player.unlockword per word.
steps.errorHandling: If shout FormID fails, skip. Some modded shouts may not be present.

steps.details: Add all armor, weapons, ammo, and misc items by FormID.
steps.errorHandling: If FormID fails (different load order), try searching by name with search_forms as fallback. Skip and log failures.

steps.details: Equip each item that was marked as equipped in the blueprint. Do this AFTER all items are added to inventory.
steps.errorHandling: Equip failures are non-critical — items remain in inventory for manual equipping.

steps.details: Set health, magicka, stamina base values. Do this LAST since perks may modify these.
steps.errorHandling: Should always succeed for base stats.

steps.details: Add gold (FormID 0000000F) with the exported count.
Add soul gems, potions, arrows, etc. with counts.
Display 'Character Blueprint Applied' on HUD. Log total successes and failures to console/file.

fallbackStrategy.description: When a modded FormID fails, try to find the item by name
process: Call search_forms with the item name
If match found with different FormID, use that instead
If no match, log as skipped and continue

knownLimitations: Enchantments on items are exported as reference data only — items added via add_item will be base (unenchanted) versions unless the FormID points to a pre-enchanted variant
Tempering level cannot be restored — items will be added at base quality
Custom-enchanted items (player-enchanted at an arcane enchanter) cannot be recreated — only pre-defined enchanted items from the game/mods
Mod FormIDs are load-order dependent — different load order = different FormIDs for modded items
ESL-flagged plugin FormIDs (FExxxxx) shift if ESL load order changes
Race-specific perks or abilities will not transfer correctly across races
Vampire/Werewolf status and associated perk trees would need separate handling
Standing Stone active effect would need separate handling
Favorited items/spells cannot be restored programmatically without MagicFavorites API integration
futureEnhancements: Enchantment recreation — use arcane enchanter API to apply known enchantments to items after adding them
Tempering recreation — use smithing API to set tempering level on added items
Follower state export (which followers are recruited, their equipment)
Player home ownership and decoration state
